quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 13 Mixed Review
quiz.subtitle: Unit 13 Quiz 11: Advanced Systems & Inequalities — Heavy Hitters (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Unit 13: Advanced Systems & Inequalities — High-End Mixed Review

questions.question: Solve the system: $x + y + z = 6$, $2x - y + z = 3$, $x + 2y - z = 2$.
questions.explanation: Solve by substitution or elimination. From first: $z = 6-x-y$. Substitute into the others: yields $x-2y=-3$ and $2x+3y=8$. Solve: $x=1, y=2, z=3$.

questions.question: Which point satisfies $y \ge 2x - 4$, $y < -x + 3$, and $x \ge 0$?
questions.explanation: Test $(1,0)$: $0 \ge -2$ ✓; $0 < 2$ ✓; $1 \ge 0$ ✓. All constraints satisfied. Other options fail at least one.

questions.question: Which point satisfies $y \le x^2 - 1$ and $y > -x - 2$?
questions.explanation: $(0,-1)$: $-1 \le -1$ ✓; $-1 > -2$ ✓. Other options fail at least one inequality.

questions.question: Find all vertices of the feasible region: $x \ge 0$, $y \ge 0$, $x + y \le 6$, $2x + y \le 8$.
questions.explanation: Intersect boundaries: $(0,0)$, $(0,6)$, $(4,0)$. Intersection of $x+y=6$ and $2x+y=8$: subtract → $x=2$, $y=4$. Feasible region vertices: $(0,0),(0,6),(2,4),(4,0)$.

questions.question: A company makes products A (2 hrs labor, 1 hr machine, $\$40$ profit) and B (1 hr labor, 3 hrs machine, $\$50$ profit). Available: 100 labor hrs, 120 machine hrs. How many of each to maximize profit?

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered Unit 13: 3×3 systems, nonlinear systems, linear and nonlinear inequalities, linear programming vertices and optimization, absolute value equations and inequalities, and real-world LP applications.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key areas to review: (1) For 3×3 systems, eliminate one variable to get a 2×2 system, (2) Nonlinear systems: set equations equal and solve, (3) Feasible region vertices come from boundary intersections, (4) For absolute value inequalities, $|ax+b| \le c$ becomes a compound inequality; $|ax+b| > c$ splits into two.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort on challenging material. Focus on: (1) Testing points in all inequalities to verify they're in the feasible region, (2) Finding all vertices of a feasible region by intersecting boundary lines, (3) Evaluating the objective function at every vertex to find max/min, (4) For LP applications, extract constraints from the problem statement carefully.
scoreMessages.0: Review Unit 13 fundamentals: (1) 3×3 systems: use elimination or substitution to reduce to 2×2, (2) Nonlinear systems: substitute or set equal, solve the resulting equation, (3) $|ax+b| = c$ → $ax+b = \pm c$, (4) $|ax+b| \le c$ → $-c \le ax+b \le c$; $|ax+b| > c$ → $ax+b > c$ or $ax+b < -c$, (5) LP: find vertices, evaluate objective function at each vertex, max/min occurs at a vertex.
